title: entada phaseoloides
intro: Entada phaseoloides, also known as the fish poison tree, is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia and Australia, characterized by its large, flat seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Entada phaseoloides include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use requires caution due to potential toxicity.
